changes_made: Consider renaming 'k8s_rbac_oidc_authentication_configured' to 'k8s_apiserver_oidc_authentication_configured' for better resource alignment
review_notes: The functions are generally well-aligned with Kubernetes security controls related to identification and authentication. However, the function 'k8s_rbac_oidc_authentication_configured' would be more accurately named 'k8s_apiserver_oidc_authentication_configured' as OIDC configuration is more directly related to the API server rather than RBAC. The other functions are correctly named and map well to the control requirements.
title: CCCS IA-2: Identification and Authentication (organizational Users)
step1_reasoning: The control CCCS IA-2 focuses on identification and authentication for organizational users. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring that authentication mechanisms are properly configured and secure. Using OIDC for authentication can provide a similar level of identity verification as MFA in cloud environments. Disabling service account token automounting helps prevent unauthorized access. Ensuring the API server has authentication enabled and uses RBAC for authorization aligns with enforcing strong access controls.
